title: Portugal vs Uzbekistan Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026- Cristiano Ronaldo scores two goals in 5-0 win - Sportstar
fetched: raw-HTML 直抓 + 抽取(apify:website-content-crawler) + 清洗（逐字、未经任何模型/改写/翻译）
original_text:
Portugal vs Uzbekistan Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026- Cristiano Ronaldo scores two goals in 5-0 win
POR vs UZB - Cristiano Ronaldo scored two goals in Portugal's 5-0 win over Uzbekistan. Enjoy all goals and highlights of that match here on Sportstar.
Updated : Jun 24, 2026 07:50 IST
Cristiano Ronaldo celebrates scoring in Portugal vs Uzbekistan in their FIFA World Cup 2026 match in Texas. | Photo Credit: REUTERS
Cristiano Ronaldo celebrates scoring in Portugal vs Uzbekistan in their FIFA World Cup 2026 match in Texas. | Photo Credit: REUTERS
elcome to Sportstar’s live coverage of the FIFA World Cup 2026 group-stage match between Portugal and Uzbekistan, which was played at the Houston Stadium in Texas. Cristiano Ronaldo and Co., who were held to a 1-1 draw with DR Congo, turned the tide in style against Uzbekistan with a commanding 5-0 win.
Ronaldo also opened his account in this edition of the tournament with two goals.
Match score: Portugal 5-0 Uzbekistan (Cristiano Ronaldo 6’, 39’, Nuno Mendes 17’, Abduvohid Nematov 60’, Rafael Leao 87’)
POR vs UZB Highlights:
6’ Pedro Neto crossed from the right and Cristiano Ronaldo shot first time to rattle the net and open his scoring in the FIFA World Cup 2026.
17’ Cristiano Ronaldo acted as the decoy as Nuno Mendes drilled the ball into the bottom-left corner with a firm left-footed hit from a free-kick
29’ Azizjon Ganiev took a stunning left-footed strike, which beat an outstretched Diogo Costa. The Uzbek fans went berserk, but their celebrations were cut short as the referee found a foul in the build up disallowed the goal.
39’ Bruno Fernandes rolled in a through ball for Ronaldo on the right. The No. 7 stayed onside and tucked it past Nematov to put Portugal 3-0 ahead.
Cristiano Ronaldo celebrates scoring twice for Portugal as his team beat Uzbekistan 5-0 in the FIFA World Cup 2026. | Photo Credit: REUTERS
60’ Nematov tried to claw the ball out from a Portugal free-kick, but his palm guided the ball into the net.
87’ Nelson Semedo’s cross from the right was received by Rafael Leão, who then found the top-left corner to complete a five-star performance for Portugal.
Portugal vs Uzbekistan Match Overview:
Kick off time: 12 noon local time (10:30 pm IST)
Venue: NRG Stadium (Houston Stadium), Houston, Texas
Referee: Jalal Jayed (Morocco)
Portugal Starting XI: Diogo Costa; João Cancelo, Rúben Dias, Renato Veiga, Nuno Mendes; João Neves, Vitinha, Bruno Fernandes; Cristiano Ronaldo. João Félix, Pedro Neto
Coach: Roberto Martinez
Uzbekistan Starting XI: Abduvohid Nematov; Abdukodir Khusanov, Abdulla Abdullaev, Rustam Ashurmatov, Behruzon Karimov, Sherzod Nasrullaev; Odiljon Xamrobekov, Azizjon Ganiev, Otabek Shukurov, Abbosbek Fayzullaev; Eldor Shomurodov
Coach: Fabio Cannavaro
Key Updates
Words from Ground Zero
FT: Portugal 5-0 Uzbekistan
87' Leao scores! Portugal 5-0 Uzbekistan
60' Own Goal by Nematov! POR 4-0 UZB
HT: Portugal 3-0 Uzbekistan
39' Goal! Cristiano Ronaldo scores again! POR 3-0 UZB
29' Ganiev scores for Uzbekistan but it is disallowed
17' Nuno Mendes scores! POR 2-0 UZB
6' Ronaldo scores for Portugal!
Confirmed line-ups: Uzbekistan
Confirmed line-ups: Portugal
June 24, 2026 01:22
That's all, folks!
And with that, we come to an end of this live blog for the Group K match between Portugal and Uzbekistan. Thank you for tuning into this space and take very good care of yourselves.
June 24, 2026 01:21
What's the next match in the FIFA World Cup?
The FIFA World Cup 2026 moves to Boston now, with England taking on Ghana. That match can be followed in the blog below:
England vs Ghana LIVE , FIFA World Cup 2026: Confirmed line-ups; Kick-off soon; Harry Kane starts
England vs Ghana Live Score: Follow ENG vs GHA match LIVE score updates, goals, lineups, standings impact and highlights from the FIFA World Cup 2026.
June 24, 2026 01:19
Words from Ground Zero
For generations of Uzbek football fans, Cristiano Ronaldo was the dream. His shirts filled the bazaars of Tashkent and Samarkand, and his celebrations were copied in every playground. On Tuesday, those supporters finally had the chance to watch their own nation face the man. For many in the stands, it was a dream and a disappointment rolled into one.
Ronaldo found his scoring boots, but the goals came against the nation that had long celebrated him from afar.
Ayon Sengupta writes about the match from the Houston Stadium in Texas below:
Ronaldo fires to get Portugal’s World Cup campaign on track in Uzbekistan hammering
Cristiano Ronaldo shines as Portugal crushes Uzbekistan 5-0, revitalizing its World Cup hopes with two goals in a dominant performance.
June 24, 2026 00:40
Portugal vs Uzbekistan player ratings:
Portugal: Costa (8.2), Cancelo (8.1), Dias (7.7), Veiga (7.3), Mendes (8.5), Neves (7.5), Vitinha (7.0), Neto (6.5), Fernandes (8.6), Joao Félix (7.3), Ronaldo (9.2)
Subs: Semedo (6.8), Conceição (7.0), Trincão (6.5), Silva (6.5), Leão (7.1)
Uzbekistan: Shomurodov (6.9), Fayzullayev (7.0), Shukurov (6.6), Khamrobekov (5.5), Ganiev (6.2), Nasrulloev (6.2), Karimov (6.5), Ashurmatov (5.0), Abdullaev (4.8), Khusanov (5.8), Nematov (4.6)
Subs: Alidzhanov (6.2), Mozgovoy (6.3), Sergeev (6.1), Esanov (No Rating), Jiyanov (No Rating)
June 24, 2026 00:29
FULL-TIME
FT: Portugal 5-0 Uzbekistan
The last action of the match is Cristiano Ronaldo getting the ball in front of goal, but the forward fails to punish the last chance, missing his hat-trick, the only thing that eluded Portugal in this dominant victory.
June 24, 2026 00:29
90+4'
Francisco Conceição makes an overlappin run along the right flank as he runs into the box and sets up Leao in the centre. Nematov makes another block.
June 24, 2026 00:21
GOAL
87' Leao scores! Portugal 5-0 Uzbekistan
Nelson Semedo gets the ball into the box and crosses to the centre from the right. Leao, who came on for Vitinha, runs in to get to the ball and shoots on target to find the top-left corner.
That’s a five-star performance for Portugal - a result it desperately needed after the upset in the opening clash.
June 24, 2026 00:20
86'
Conceicao tries to make a run along the right flank this time, but Uzbekistan stops the players in his tracks.
June 24, 2026 00:18
84'
Portugal tries to go for another attack along the left flank, with Leao trying to shoot. He looses the ball and Bruno goes for goal from distance, prompting another save by Nematov.
June 24, 2026 00:15
80'
Joao Neves gets the ball in his half and then helps the ball get into the final third. Though Uzbekistan gets the ball back, Neves again wins the ball back and passes to Vitinha, whose attempt to trigger an overlap ends to nothing.
June 24, 2026 00:13
78' Chance for Uzbekistan!
Shomurodov gets a cross from the left. He receives it with his left foot and shoots with his right. But the ball flies just over the net. What a chance that was for the debutants!
June 24, 2026 00:09
74'
Ronaldo then forces a mistake by Nematov, winning the ball in the opposition’s penalty box. He controls the ball well and then takes a shot, which is saved by Nematov with excellent reflexes.
June 24, 2026 00:07
72'
The match resumes after the second hydration break and Portugal looks to play out from the back. Uzbekistan then loses the ball in its half and then Nuno Mendes gets the ball into the final third and crosses to Cristiano Ronaldo, whose shot is deflected out for a corner.
June 24, 2026 00:03
69' Second hydration break!
The referee pauses play for the second hydration break! And resounding boos from fans echo around the stadium. Hydration breaks have been a major talking point in this World Cup, with claims of the break slowing momentum down of games.
June 24, 2026 00:02
68' Plenty of records broken by Ronaldo today
Cristiano Ronaldo became the first player to score in six FIFA World Cup editions after netting for Portugal in its Group K match against Uzbekistan in Houston on Tuesday.
Cristiano Ronaldo becomes first player to score in six FIFA World Cup editions
Cristiano Ronaldo makes history as the first player to score in six FIFA World Cup editions with a goal against Uzbekistan.
June 24, 2026 00:00
66'
Portugal looks to have shifted from a 4-3-3 to a 4-5-1 shape, with Ronaldo up ahead. Nuno Mendes and Semedo continue making forward runs up the pitch, but Uzbekistan is more in damage-control mode than looking to attack.
June 23, 2026 23:58
64'
The Portugal side today looks a starkly different one to the team that started against DR Congo. Two free-kicks with Ronaldo as a decoy instead of the FK taker and still two goals to his name - Portugal looks on song today.
June 23, 2026 23:54
GOAL
60' Own Goal by Nematov! POR 4-0 UZB
Portugal tries another set-piece routine through a corner kick. Bruno takes the corner, which is kicked by Ronaldo. The ball gets a touch off Khusanov and as Nematov tries to claw it out, his palm guides the ball into the net.
The day gets bad to worse for Uzbekistan. Two matches, seven goals in.
June 23, 2026 23:53
58' Chance for Ronaldo!
Cristiano Ronaldo makes leaves it for Bruno, who lobs it for the Ronaldo into the box. The Al Nassr strike’s shot is stopped by Nematov. Portugal thinks there is a chance of a penalty but the referee is not interested.
June 23, 2026 23:51
57'
Cristiano Ronaldo is fouled in front of the penalty box, and Portugal gets a free-kick. Will Ronaldo take it now? Or is another ploy in action? We will have to wait and watch.
June 23, 2026 23:49
55'
Alidzhanov passes to Fayzullaev, who gets the ball to Shukurov. The No. 7 tries a shot from a distance but fails to trouble the goalkeeper.
June 23, 2026 23:48
52'
Seconds later, Alidzhanov makes a run from his own half into the final third of Portugal and passes the ball to Fayzullaev, who takes a right-footed hit. And the ball is caught comfortably by Diogo Costa.
June 23, 2026 23:46
51'
A moment to compose and play from the back for Portugal. The ball reaches the goalkeeper Nematov as Uzbekistan wins the ball back and Ronaldo presses the goalie, almost forcing an error.
June 23, 2026 23:43
49'
Semedo tries to drag the Uzbek side out of its defensive shape as the Asian side looks to stop the bleeding with five defenders back in and around their box. The team had started with overlapping full-backs but its high line has come back to bite in the back.
June 23, 2026 23:41
47'
Portugal is looking much more sure-footed as it looks to pile on more misery on Uzbekistan. Nuno Mendes passes to Joao Felix, who fails to break into the box and then tries a shot from distance. The ball goes inches over the woodwork.
June 23, 2026 23:39
Second half begins!
Portugal starts with two changes after the break, with Semedo and Conceicao coming on for Joao Cancelo and Pedro Neto.
June 23, 2026 23:21
HT: Portugal 3-0 Uzbekistan
The last action of the first half sees Cristiano Ronaldo get a cross in the box. He looked off-side and his blocked shot, which was heading for goal, is cleared on the line by Khusanov.
June 23, 2026 23:19
45+3'
For coach Fabio Cannavaro, there will be plenty to think about in the break. The World Cup which he won 20 years ago had a lean quick forward playing for Portugal with ‘Ronaldo’ written on his jersey. That boy has grown up now and how!
June 23, 2026 23:16
45+2'
Portugal came into this game with intent, and the 3-0 scoreline in the first 45 minutes reflects it aptly. The Nations League champion now looks to run down the stoppage time to regroup and look ahead for the second half.
June 23, 2026 23:15
43'
Mendes loses the ball to Karimov along the left flank and Ganiev sets up Shomurodov, who then passes to Shukurov. Shukurov fails to cause any trouble for Diogo Costa or Portugal.
June 23, 2026 23:12
42'
Vitinha lobs in a long ball for Joao Felix on the left and the Al Nassr forward tries to take a shot the first time. However, the ball goes flying over the net.
June 23, 2026 23:08
39' Goal! Cristiano Ronaldo scores again! POR 3-0 UZB
Bruno Fernandes carries the ball from Portugal’s half into the final third of Uzbekistan and rolls in a through ball for Ronaldo on the right. The No. 7 stays just onside and then tucks it past Nematov to put Portugal 3-0 ahead.
Portugal's Cristiano Ronaldo scores his side's third goal past Uzbekistan goalkeeper Abduvohid Nematov during the World Cup Group K soccer match between Portugal and Uzbekistan in Houston, Tuesday, June 23, 2026. (AP Photo/Ashley Landis)
Portugal's Cristiano Ronaldo scores his side's third goal past Uzbekistan goalkeeper Abduvohid Nematov during the World Cup Group K soccer match between Portugal and Uzbekistan in Houston, Tuesday, June 23, 2026. (AP Photo/Ashley Landis) | Photo Credit: Ashley Landis
June 23, 2026 23:07
36'
The tempo match keeps on skewing towards Portugal as it launches overloads, one after the other in the Uzbek half. Shomurodov, wins the ball in the midfield, darts into the final third. He tries to pass to Ganiev on his right, but Nuno Mendes makes a timely interception.
June 23, 2026 22:59
FREE KICK
29' Ganiev scores for Uzbekistan but it is disallowed
Azizjon Ganiev tries to win the ball from Joao Cancelo as the ball rolls to Azizjon Ganiev. Ganiev takes a stunning left-footed strike, which beats an outstretched Diogo Costa. The Uzbek fans go berserk, but their celebrations are cut short as the referee goes for a VAR check.
HOUSTON, TEXAS - JUNE 23: Diogo Costa #1 of Portugal dives in vain as Azizjon Ganiev #19 (not pictured) of Uzbekistan scores the team's first goal later disallowed by a foul committed by Abbosbek Fayzullaev #22 (not pictured) during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group K match between Portugal and Uzbekistan at Houston Stadium on June 23, 2026 in Houston, Texas. Francois Nel/Getty Images/AFP (Photo by Francois Nel / GETTY IMAGES NORTH AMERICA / Getty Images via AFP)
HOUSTON, TEXAS - JUNE 23: Diogo Costa #1 of Portugal dives in vain as Azizjon Ganiev #19 (not pictured) of Uzbekistan scores the team's first goal later disallowed by a foul committed by Abbosbek Fayzullaev #22 (not pictured) during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group K match between Portugal and Uzbekistan at Houston Stadium on June 23, 2026 in Houston, Texas. Francois Nel/Getty Images/AFP (Photo by Francois Nel / GETTY IMAGES NORTH AMERICA / Getty Images via AFP) | Photo Credit: FRANCOIS NEL
The VAR check reveals that Fayzullaev had not got a contact on the ball first as a result of that - a foul in the build up - the goal is disallowed.
June 23, 2026 22:59
28'
As the match resumes after the hydration break, Uzbekistan shows promise with Nasrulloev crossing from the left for Shomudorov. Ruben Dias says, ‘Not on my watch’ as he clears the ball away.
June 23, 2026 22:55
25'
If you have logged into this space just now, there have been two goals in the match so far, with Cristiano Ronaldo scoring his first in this edition of the tournament, and Nuno Mendes netting the next, with Ronaldo as the decoy.
June 23, 2026 22:54
23'
Cancelo marks Shomudorov and the Uzbek forward goes down appealing for a free-kick. The referee, however, rules for a hydration break instead.
June 23, 2026 22:51
21'
Uzbekistan concedes another free-kick close to the penalty box. Vitinha, Bruno and Mendes take their positions for the free-kick. Bruno passes to Mendes, who crosses to the centre. But Uzbekistan manages to get the ball away.
June 23, 2026 22:49
19'
Uzbekistan tries to find a quick response with an attack through the left flank. Nasrulloev gets the ball in the box and goes for goal, forcing a good save by Diogo Costa.
June 23, 2026 22:46
17' Nuno Mendes scores! POR 2-0 UZB
Cristiano Ronaldo steps up for the free-kick after Pedro Neto is fouled by Odildzhon Khammrobekov. But the trap works. It is not Ronaldo, but Nuno Mendes who drills the ball into the net with a firm left-footed hit.
June 23, 2026 22:44
13'
Pedro Neto and Joao Neves try to scratch the surface of Uzbekistan’s defence, but the Asian side has five alert men stationed at the back. The team must know a bit or two about defending for its coach Fabio Cannavaro - one of the few defenders to win the Ballon d’Or.
June 23, 2026 22:41
11'
Bruno Fernandes takes the free-kick, which is palmed away by the Uzbek goalkeeper. The balls falls to Cancelo, who takes the shot from outside the box, but is blocked by Uzbek’s defencve.
June 23, 2026 22:40
9'
Bruno Fernandes passes to Nuno Mendes on the left, who tries a cross but Khusanov blocks it. Mendes takes the throw in, getting it to Felix, but Karimov fouls the Al Nassr forward for a Portugal free-kick.
June 23, 2026 22:36
GOAL
6' Ronaldo scores for Portugal!
Portugal now tries a set-piece attack from the right as Joao Cancelo cuts back from the right and Cristiano Ronaldo shoots first time and rattles the net to open his scoring in the FIFA World Cup 2026.
June 23, 2026 22:35
4' Miss by Ronaldo!
Portugal goes for another attack, with Nuno Mendes whipping in a cross from the left flank for Cristiano Ronaldo. The forward fails to get the perfect connection on the ball as a major chance goes begging for Portugal to take an early lead.
June 23, 2026 22:34
3'
Portugal takes the corner as a set-piece routine of small passes but Shukurov ensures the ball is cleared away.
June 23, 2026 22:33
2'
Joao Neves passes to Bruno Fernandes, who then sends in a cross to the centre from the left, but Uzbeks clear it only temporarily. Bruno then gets a pass in the box, he turns and takes a left-footed shot, which is blocked out for a corner kick.
June 23, 2026 22:31
1'
Uzbekistan starts in a 5-4-1 shape, playing from left to right, while Portugal has chosen a conventional counterattacking 4-3-3, with Cristiano Ronaldo at the centre of the attacking puzzle.
June 23, 2026 22:30
Kick Off!
The Group K match kicks off between Portugal and Uzbekistan in Houston, with Portugal starting in its conventional red kit, while the Uzbeks start in white.
June 23, 2026 22:26
A note of remembrance for Diogo Jota
The Portugal national anthem ends on a sombre note, remembering former striker Diogo Jota, who lost his life in a tragic car accident last year.
June 23, 2026 22:22
Minutes to kick off!
The players are ready in the tunnel, set to walk out in front of a packed crowd, as Portugal takes on debutant Uzbekistan in a few minutes. Stay tuned here for all the real-time updates.
June 23, 2026 22:20
Form guide: Uzbekistan
Uzbekistan’s form, going into this game, is starkly different to Portugal. It has lost three of its last four matches and its last win game against Gabon in March - a 3-1 victory where Eldor Shomurodov had found the net. The former Roma striker starts today and will hope to conjure a historic win in Houston.
June 23, 2026 22:10
Pressure continues to build on Cristiano Ronaldo
Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo have dominated football discussions for the last two decades and have 13 Ballon d’Or trophies between them. While Messi has scored a brace in each of his games in the FIFA World Cup so far, it has been a disappointing show for the former Manchester United and Real Madrid forward, with no goals or assists to his name so far in the tournament.
Facing lower-ranked Uzbekistan, which is playing in the World Cup for the first time, Ronaldo will look to end earn his spot back into the thick of things about football and goals in fan circles.
June 23, 2026 22:06
Form guide: Portugal
Portugal is unbeaten in its last five matches, having won thrice and drawn two games, including a 1-1 draw with DR Congo. Its last defeat dates back to November 2025, when it lost 0-2 to Ireland where Cristiano Ronaldo was sent off.
June 23, 2026 21:58
Time for Cristiano Ronaldo to end the drought?
During Portugal’s opening 1-1 draw with DR Congo, Cristiano Ronaldo played all 90 minutes but managed just 25 touches. This was the lowest active involvement he has recorded in any of his 43 major international tournament matches where he played at least 70 minutes.
And, with Lionel Messi, Erling Haaland and Kylian Mbappe scoring twice in each of the last two rounds, the pressure gets back to Ronaldo to find the net sooner rather than later.
June 23, 2026 21:44
A World Cup legend on Uzbekistan bench
Uzbekistan has Ballon d’Or winner Fabio Cannavaro on its bench as its head coach. Two decades later, the Italian had guided his country to the World Cup. He will look to inspire a similar tale with debutant Uzbekistan this time around.
June 23, 2026 21:38
A perfectly blank slate
Uzbekistan and Portugal have never met in men’s senior football before, either in friendlies or competitive fixtures. Who will Lady Fortune smile on today? We will have to watch.
June 23, 2026 21:19
Teammates against each other
Manchester City will have a battle within its walls today, with Abdukodir Khusanov starting for Uzbekistan in defence against Dias for Portugal. On the biggest stage of international football, the two will look to forget their club allegiances with an important win up for grabs in Houston.
June 23, 2026 21:12
A major return in defence for Portugal
Portugal coach Roberto Martinez has decided to bring back Ruben Dias in the back four. The Manchester City centre-back was out with an injury and returned to training shortly after the match against DR Congo, and he is expected to play a leader’s role to prevent Uzbeks from scoring.
June 23, 2026 21:09
Confirmed line-ups: Uzbekistan
Abduvohid Nematov; Abdukodir Khusanov, Abdulla Abdullaev, Rustam Ashurmatov, Behruzon Karimov, Sherzod Nasrullaev; Odiljon Xamrobekov, Azizjon Ganiev, Otabek Shukurov, Abbosbek Fayzullaev; Eldor Shomurodov
June 23, 2026 21:06
Confirmed line-ups: Portugal
Diogo Costa; João Cancelo, Rúben Dias, Renato Veiga, Nuno Mendes; Joao Neves, Vitinha, Bruno Fernandes; Cristiano Ronaldo. Joao Felix, Pedro Neto
June 23, 2026 21:02
Match preview
For a team that enjoyed 75 per cent possession, completed 783 passes and spent much of the afternoon monopolising the ball, Portugal emerged from its World Cup opener with remarkably little to show for it.
The numbers from the 1-1 draw against DR Congo make for uncomfortable reading as Portugal managed just seven shots, with only one of them on target. DR Congo, despite seeing only a quarter of the ball, produced eight shots and had a superior xG of 0.87 compared to Portugal’s 0.65.
The result inevitably has brought the focus on Cristiano Ronaldo.
Published on Jun 23, 2026
